evals.prompt: 我上传了一张柠檬水瓶和冰咖啡杯的照片，帮我用喜茶那种风格分别做带字版和不带字版。带字版要有歪歪扭扭的儿童字，没字版不要出现任何文字。
evals.expected_output: A two-template response that treats the typography-led version and no-text narrative version as separate concepts, not the same layout with text toggled.
expectations: The output offers or uses the three template choices: 带字版, 无字版, 两套都出（推荐）.
If private-assets/reference-cutouts/asset-index.json exists, the output lists separate selected reference assets for each template.
The 带字版 prompt makes rough crooked childlike Chinese marker lettering the first visual priority.
The 带字版 selects or describes a primary lettering/title-blocks/* cutout as the title rhythm reference.
The 带字版 uses or recommends lettering/glyphs/* and lettering/strokes/* references when the title must be more faithful.
The 带字版 uses or recommends separate poster-base, title construction reference sheet, and title-layer prompts for publishable output instead of only one all-in-one prompt.
The 无字版 prompt forbids all invented text, letters, numbers, captions, labels, and glyphs.
The 无字版 selects or describes a primary figures/full-poses/* cutout and optionally a figures/action-parts/* cutout, and does not select any lettering/* cutout.
The two versions use different composition priorities instead of sharing one layout.
Both versions preserve the real cup and bottle as photographic objects.
Both versions use primitive black-line micro workers rather than cute cartoon characters.

evals.prompt: 这张甜品照片做一版带字的，重点还原参考里那种左上角粗黑、歪扭、小孩写坏了的中文标题。小人可以有，但不要抢标题。
evals.expected_output: A typography-led poster direction where the rough crooked title dominates the composition and the dessert plus micro worker support the title.
expectations: The output selects or clearly follows the 带字版 template.
If reference cutouts are available, the output uses or lists a primary lettering/title-blocks/* reference for title rhythm.
If the user emphasizes font fidelity, the output creates or recommends a title construction reference sheet before the title layer.
The title is described as large, rough, crooked, childlike, heavy black Chinese marker lettering.
The title treatment rejects perfect 2x2 grid placement and asks for loose staggered vertical columns when the title has four characters.
The real object is placed smaller or lower so it supports the title.
The micro worker action is restrained and does not compete with the lettering.
The prompt rejects clean fonts, rounded cute handwriting, neat calligraphy, and sans-serif text.

evals.prompt: 我想要无字版，照片是一杯茶和茶包。不要留标题空位，不要任何字，只靠小人动作和附带元素讲故事。
evals.expected_output: A no-text narrative poster direction with no title block, no invented text, and an action-led micro-worker scene around the tea object.
expectations: The output selects or clearly follows the 无字版 template.
If reference cutouts are available, the output uses or lists a primary figures/full-poses/* reference that matches the action.
The output does not use or list lettering references for the 无字版.
The prompt explicitly forbids all text, letters, numbers, labels, captions, and random glyphs.
The composition does not reserve a title area.
The micro worker action is more explicit and narrative than in the typography-led template.
Supporting elements are limited to action-serving marks such as a liquid arc, drops, ladder, bucket, bag, arrow, thought bubble, or ground line.

evals.prompt: 我这张生活物件照片背景很乱，想做成喜茶感白底海报。你先告诉我应该用带字版还是无字版，或者两套都出。
evals.expected_output: A workflow that first frames the template choice, defaults to two independent concepts if the user wants immediate output, and explains how each template would handle the same object differently.
expectations: The output explains the template choice before generation.
If reference cutouts are available, the output explains that 带字版 and 无字版 need different reference asset sets.
The default recommendation is 两套都出（推荐） when the user has not chosen.
The 带字版 concept uses typography-led composition.
The 无字版 concept uses action-led composition with no title area.
The output first isolates one primary real object from the busy background.

evals.prompt: 字体还是太像普通电脑字了。你不要重新跑整张图，重点想办法还原那种歪歪扭扭的小孩写坏了的中文标题。
evals.expected_output: A correction workflow that keeps the poster base, builds or uses a title construction reference sheet, and regenerates only the title layer.
expectations: The output does not recommend retrying the full poster as the main fix.
The output identifies the failure as a glyph-skeleton problem, not only a texture or prompt-adjective problem.
The output uses or recommends lettering/title-blocks/* for title rhythm.
The output uses or recommends lettering/glyphs/* and lettering/strokes/* for malformed glyph anatomy.
The output builds or references a title construction reference sheet before regenerating the title layer.
The output says to keep the existing poster base unchanged and iterate only the title layer.
The revision prompt rejects clean font skeletons, neat grids, calligraphy rhythm, rounded handwriting, and standard sans-serif typography.

evals.prompt: 准备把这个 skill push 上去之前，帮我检查公开包里有没有不该带的本地素材、路径或旧脚本。
evals.expected_output: A release-boundary review that treats private reference assets as local-only and checks the package contents before push.
expectations: The output checks for local absolute workstation paths and private cached source references in public files.
The output confirms private-assets/reference-cutouts/ can be included when reference images are intended to ship.
The output flags raw source caches and scripts/extract_reference_assets.py as private-only if they appear in the public package.
The output verifies that scripts/build_title_reference_sheet.py and scripts/composite_title_layer.py are present when public docs mention them.
The output warns against staging .DS_Store, stale generated packages, or unrelated generated-skills output.
The output treats package cleanliness as a release gate, not as a visual-style preference.
